title: MCP tool output too large — summarize before retrying
symptom: An MCP tool call returns an error about output being too large, truncated, or exceeding maximum response size. The tool may have succeeded but the result cannot be fully returned.
applies_when: MCP tool returned a large result (>100KB)
Tool output was truncated or caused a context overflow
not_this_if: Tool actually failed (not an output size issue)
Error is about input being too large
root_cause: MCP tools have output size limits. When a tool returns a very large result (e.g., listing all files in a large directory, fetching a full database table, or reading a very long file), the output may be truncated or rejected entirely.
fix_steps: Add filters or limits to the tool call to reduce output size
Use pagination if the tool supports it
Request a summary or count instead of full data
If reading a file, use offset/limit parameters to read a specific section
agent_instruction: The MCP tool output was too large. Retry with filters, limits, or pagination to reduce output size. Request only the specific data needed, not the full result.
version_notes: Output size limits may vary by MCP server implementation
